name: SpectraGlow premium pico laser
shortDescription: PicoSecond ND:YAG laser with 2000ps pulse. 1600 mJ energy, 2–10 mm spot size, tattoo removal in as few as 3–4 sessions.
description:
The SpectraGlow premium pico laser represents the most advanced aesthetic laser technology, working with ultra-short, picosecond pulses.

Compared to traditional Q-Switch lasers, it breaks pigments into much smaller particles, allowing the body to remove them faster and more efficiently. This requires fewer treatments while the skin suffers less damage, healing is faster and less painful.

The 1064 nm and 532 nm wavelengths (optionally 585 nm and 650 nm) cover the full pigment spectrum from dark tattoo removal through red pigments to skin rejuvenation.
metaTitle: SpectraGlow premium pico laser | Tattoo, pigment, skin rejuvenation
metaDescription: SpectraGlow PicoSecond ND:YAG laser with 2000ps pulse. Tattoo removal in 3–4 sessions, pigment treatment, skin rejuvenation. 1600 mJ energy, 2-year warranty.

videoDescription.headline: SpectraGlow – The pinnacle of picosecond technology
paragraphs: The SpectraGlow is not simply another laser – it is the most advanced technology for pigment removal and skin rejuvenation. Picosecond pulses break down pigment particles in trillionths of a second, far surpassing the efficiency of traditional nanosecond lasers.
It works on two base wavelengths (1064 nm and 532 nm), and can optionally be expanded with 585 nm and 650 nm wavelengths – treating virtually every colour of tattoo and pigmentation.
The 2–10 mm adjustable spot size and 1–10 Hz frequency enable precise, personalised treatment for every area.

featureRows.headline: Why is the SpectraGlow the best investment?
featureRows.subtitle: Picosecond technology that revolutionises your treatments

features.title: Picosecond pulse – faster and more effective
features.description:
The 2000 picosecond pulse duration breaks down pigment particles in trillionths of a second, which the body absorbs and removes much more easily. Compared to traditional nanosecond lasers, fewer treatments are needed and the skin suffers less damage.

The picosecond advantage:

Smaller pigment particles = faster absorption
Fewer treatments needed (tattoo: 3–4 sessions)
Minimal skin damage, faster healing
features.highlight: The SpectraGlow's picosecond pulse can reduce the number of required treatments by up to half.

features.title: Full pigment spectrum – for every tattoo colour
features.description:
The 1064 nm wavelength is effective on dark pigments (black, blue, grey, green), while the 532 nm targets warm shades (red, orange, brown). Optionally expandable with 585 nm and 650 nm wavelengths for full colour spectrum coverage.

Wavelengths and their applications:

1064 nm – black, blue, grey, green pigments
532 nm – red, orange, warm brown pigments
585 nm (optional) – special pigments
650 nm (optional) – supplementary spectrum
features.highlight: With optional wavelengths, the SpectraGlow can treat virtually every pigment colour.

features.title: Tattoo removal – in fewer sessions
features.description:
The main advantage of picosecond technology shows in tattoo removal: while traditional Q-switched lasers require 6–12 sessions, the SpectraGlow can remove tattoos in as few as 3–4 sessions.
Body tattoo removal
Permanent makeup correction
Multi-layered tattoos
Professional and amateur work

features.title: Skin rejuvenation and pigment treatment
features.description:
The SpectraGlow is not only for tattoo removal – the collagen-stimulating effect of picosecond pulses effectively treats signs of skin ageing.
Pigmentation spot fading
Skin tightening and collagen stimulation
Acne scar fading
Skin tone improvement
features.highlight: Picosecond pulses also stimulate collagen production – resulting in younger, firmer skin.

features.title: Professional technical specifications
features.description:
The SpectraGlow is a 113 kg, 90x45x170 cm professional standing unit that provides reliable performance for the highest level treatments.

Key specifications:

Pulse energy: max. 1600 mJ (double)
Pulse duration: 2000 ps
Frequency: 1–10 Hz
Spot size: 2–10 mm

training.headline: Device operator training – premium pico laser course
training.intro: An official, FAR-registered device operator training is available for the SpectraGlow premium pico laser. The curriculum was developed with the collaboration of healthcare professionals and laser specialist physicians, and includes the special treatment protocols of picosecond technology.
items: Theoretical foundations and operating principles of picosecond laser technology
Differences between nanosecond (Q-switched) and picosecond lasers
Wavelengths and application areas (tattoo, pigment, skin rejuvenation)
Treatment protocols for different pigment types and skin conditions
Contraindications, skin type assessment and safe treatment
closingParagraphs: The aim of the training is to enable participants to confidently and safely apply the SpectraGlow pico laser for even the most demanding treatments.
Upon completion of the training, an official FAR certificate will be issued.

testimonials.name: Krisztina Balogh
testimonials.text: I was initially sceptical about the pico laser price tag, but the results convinced me. <mark>Pigmentation treatment and skin rejuvenation deliver fantastic results</mark>, my clients love it. The device paid for itself in the first six months.

faq.question: How does the pico laser differ from a traditional Q-switched laser?
faq.answer: The main difference lies in the pulse duration: the pico laser works in picoseconds (trillionths of a second), while the Q-switched works in nanoseconds (billionths of a second). The shorter pulse breaks the pigment into much smaller particles, which the body absorbs more easily and quickly. Result: fewer treatments, faster healing, less skin stress.

faq.question: How many treatments are needed for tattoo removal?
faq.answer: With the SpectraGlow pico laser, an average of 3–4 treatments is sufficient for most tattoo removals, compared to the 6–12 session protocol of traditional lasers. The exact number depends on the tattoo's size, colour, depth and skin type.

faq.question: What tattoo colours can it treat?
faq.answer: The 1064 nm wavelength is effective on dark pigments (black, blue, grey, green), the 532 nm on warm colours (red, orange, brown). Optionally, with 585 nm and 650 nm wavelengths, the full colour spectrum can be covered.

faq.question: Is the treatment painful?
faq.answer: Picosecond pulses stress the skin less than nanosecond lasers, so the treatment is generally less painful. Anaesthesia is usually not needed, but topical anaesthetic cream can be applied if desired.

faq.question: What skin types is it suitable for?
faq.answer: The SpectraGlow can be safely used on all skin types (Fitzpatrick I–VI). Picosecond technology minimises thermal effects, reducing the risk of side effects even on darker skin types.

faq.question: How quickly does the SpectraGlow pay for itself?
faq.answer: Tattoo removal costs 30,000–80,000 Ft per treatment, pigment treatment 20,000–50,000 Ft, skin rejuvenation 25,000–60,000 Ft. With 3–5 clients per day, the SpectraGlow typically pays for itself in 4–6 months. Use our ROI calculator for an exact calculation!

faq.question: Can I see the device before purchasing?
faq.answer: Yes! You can personally view and try out the SpectraGlow pico laser in our showroom (Érd, Budai út 28.) by prior appointment.
